creator: bugzilla@soulrebel.in-berlin.de
text:
When I make excessive use of 3D-accleration, e.g. run games/nexuiz, games/glest
games/linux-nwnclient, FreeBSD freezes, the last mseconds of sound looping 
(kind of like the thing you would be used to as a windows user ;) ).
The freeze happens anywhere between 30seconds in-game to 20minutes in-game, 
although it crashes mostly in the first 5 minutes. The system is completely
frozen, no panic-message, no nothing.

I have updated to RELENG_7 a few days ago, the problem existed before as well, 
but I remember some time back in the 6.0 or 6.1-times where FreeBSD did not 
crash with 3D-apps.

I deactivated geli and manually set dumpdir in /etc/rc.conf; I even
compiled the kernel with debug-symbols and activated KDB, but I don't get
any Kernel-Dumps.

I setup a DracoGNU/Linux to check for hardware issues. The problems cannot be 
reproduced with Linux.

Maybe this is related to http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=127436

Below is all information I deem relevant, if you need more, please let me know.

Thank you fro your help.

------------------------------------

%dmesg | grep -i drm
drm0: <Intel i945GM> on vgapci0
info: [drm] AGP at 0xd0000000 256MB
info: [drm] Initialized i915 1.5.0 20060119
drm0: [ITHREAD]
%dmesg | grep -i agp
agp0: <Intel 82945GM (945GM GMCH) SVGA controller> on vgapci0
agp0: detected 7932k stolen memory
agp0: aperture size is 256M
info: [drm] AGP at 0xd0000000 256MB


-------------------------------------

xorg.conf:
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Card0"
        Driver      "intel"
        VendorName  "Intel Corporation"
        BoardName   "Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        BusID       "PCI:0:2:0"
        Option "Accel" "True"
        Option "AccelMethod" "EXA" 
        Option "ExaNoComposite" "false" 
        Option "MigrationHeuristic" "greedy"
EndSection

---------------------------------------

An Xorg.0.log is available @
http://www.soulrebel.in-berlin.de/pub/Xorg.0.log

How-To-Repeat: run one of the named games.
Note that glxgears and other "smaller" apps do not cause a crash.
